etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") + phonetic 昜(昜) (yáng) -> open area on earth
etymology.interpretation: The character 場 (chǎng, "field; place; venue") pairs 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") suggesting a physical space or ground, with 昜(昜) (yáng) as the phonetic component. This combination conveys the idea of an open area that is associated with land or ground, leading to the meaning of a 'field' or 'place'.

examples.chinese: 我们去篮球场打球(我們去籃球場打球)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen qù lánqiú chǎng dǎ qiú.
examples.english: We go to the basketball court to play.

examples.chinese: 电影开场后(電影開場後)，大家都很安静(大家都很安靜)。
examples.pinyin: Diànyǐng kāichǎng hòu, dàjiā dōu hěn ānjìng.
examples.english: After the movie started, everyone was very quiet.

examples.chinese: 他在足球场上踢得很好(他在足球場上踢得很好)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài zúqiú chǎng shàng tī de hěn hǎo.
examples.english: He plays very well on the soccer field.
